title: Sagittaria tenella
taxonomy: Sagittaria tenella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Alismataceae. It belongs to the genus Sagittaria, which consists of various aquatic and semi-aquatic plants. The family Alismataceae is a group of monocots commonly known as water plantains or shoalwaters. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf and flower morphology. Within the genus Sagittaria, S. tenella is one of the many species found in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Sagittaria tenella, also known as dwarf arrowhead, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's underground rhizomes are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. It is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating infections and wounds. In addition, the plant's leaves and stems are used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and indigestion. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Sagittaria tenella for medicinal purposes.

properties: Sagittaria tenella has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. constituents: Sagittaria tenella contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids.
